problem_statement:
You are given an n Ã n matrix grid where grid[i][j] is the unique elevation of cell (i, j).
Rain starts falling at time t = 0.
At any later time t â¥ 0, every cell is covered by water to depth t.
You may move 4-directionally (up, down, left, right) between adjacent cells instantaneously iff the elevations of both cells are â¤ t.
Starting from the top-left cell (0, 0), return the minimum time t at which you can reach the bottom-right cell (n â 1, n â 1).

examples.explanation:
t = 0, only (0,0) is reachable.  
t = 1, cells {0,0},{1,0} reachable, but (1,1) still blocked (elevation 3).  
t = 2, cells {0,0},{0,1},{1,0} reachable.  
t = 3, all cells â¤ 3 are flooded, so we can swim to (1,1). Minimum t = 3.
The earliest moment the start and end cells belong to the same flooded component is t = 16.
